title: Desperate for shade on your walk? There’s (almost) an app for that.
content: By mapping shade, a new online tool calculates the best way to stroll a city without overheating.
snippet: By mapping shade, a new online tool calculates the best way to stroll a city without overheating.

title: Breaking the debt–climate trap in Asia and the Pacific
content: Developing economies in Asia and the Pacific are caught in a dangerous feedback loop: rising debt burdens are constraining their ability to invest in climate action, while climate shocks are worsening fiscal pressures and increasing debt risks.

title: Multi-hazard early warnings for all: Roadmap for Samoa
content: The Multi-Hazard Early Warnings for All (EW4All) Roadmap for Samoa 2026–2036 outlines Samoa’s strategy for strengthening its national early warning systems to better prepare for and respond to natural hazards and climate-related disasters.

title: Rapid disaster damage assessment using deep adversarial sliced Wasserstein domain adaptation
content: This study investigates the use of unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) techniques for rapid damage assessment during disasters.
snippet: This study investigates the use of unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) techniques for rapid damage assessment during disasters.

title: Loss and damage beyond economics: exploring health as a non-economic loss in national climate planning
content: The report offers a snapshot of how health-related Loss and Damage is reflected in national climate policy documents, examining the extent to which physical health, mental health, and health systems are integrated into climate planning and action.

title: Standing the heat: an analysis of heatwave financing in India’s Union budget
content: This report highlights a significant gap in dedicated public financing for heat resilience In India.
snippet: This report highlights a significant gap in dedicated public financing for heat resilience In India.

title: Italy’s Cinque Terre coastline could be hit by 13-metre waves by 2150 as sea levels rise
content: Beaches, ports and, during extreme events, even the railway line that runs through the Cinque Terre National Park are at risk.
snippet: Beaches, ports and, during extreme events, even the railway line that runs through the Cinque Terre National Park are at risk.

title: Deep learning completes US flood hazard maps revealing millions exposed to previously unrecognized risk
content: This paper presents a deep-learning framework that learns from existing records to produce a spatially complete 30-m flood hazard map for the contiguous US, filling unmapped regions and updating legacy extents.

title: Seismic capacity survey and earthquake risk assessment of self-built buildings in rural areas of Shanghai Municipality
content: This study conducted field research and theoretical calculations to gain an overall understanding of the seismic performance of these buildings.
snippet: This study conducted field research and theoretical calculations to gain an overall understanding of the seismic performance of these buildings.

title: Rapid assessment of flood-induced agricultural losses to support infrastructure and policy decisions
content: This study develops an integrated, stochastic modeling framework to quantify three distinct agricultural flood-damage aspects—prevented planting, unharvested acreage, and yield loss—in Chariton County, Missouri.

title: Preventing future zoonotic pandemics: strengthening national legal frameworks & international cooperation

title: UK rivers face rising risk of climate ‘whiplash’
content: Climate change could push UK rivers to dangerous extremes and see more frequent rapid swings between wet and dry conditions – a phenomenon known as hydroclimatic whiplash – according to research led by the University of East Anglia (UEA).

title: Brown researchers launch tool to map World Cup players, fans and potential to spread disease
content: Using data from FIFA, Brown epidemiologists developed a tracking tool aimed at assisting public health experts in the event of an infectious disease outbreak.
snippet: Using data from FIFA, Brown epidemiologists developed a tracking tool aimed at assisting public health experts in the event of an infectious disease outbreak.

title: Philippines holds nationwide earthquake drill amid rising seismic activities, volcanic unrest

title: Inclusive climate risk finance: the idea is right, the execution is hard

title: Rwanda strengthens Ebola preparedness in protected areas

title: Government changes in Bangladesh and West Bengal could impact river treaties

title: Floating volcanic rock is disrupting life in Papua New Guinea – and the problem will last a long time

title: When multiple hazards interact and the data doesn’t: The multi-hazard modelling problem nobody wants to talk about

title: Heat waves increase wildfire risk – a new study explains how much, and it’s not a small number

title: A ‘super’ El Niño has the power to devastate fishing – and leave seals and sea lions starving
content: This is defined as the strongest El Niño event you can get, and happens when sea surface temperatures in the tropical Pacific Ocean rise by more than 2°C.
snippet: This is defined as the strongest El Niño event you can get, and happens when sea surface temperatures in the tropical Pacific Ocean rise by more than 2°C.
